summary: Iteration 2 (Modo Afiliado MANUAL + JWT auth) tested end-to-end. Backend: 25/25 pytest cases passed (auth, affiliates CRUD, sales CRUD with commission calc, role-based filtering of /api/sales, stats/me, stats/network, plus iter-1 regressions: root, status, /command real OpenAI, WhatsApp/Telegram webhook auth). Frontend: 9/9 critical UI flows passed (login error legible, admin login -> AdminDashboard with tabs, KPIs visibles, crear afiliado, registrar venta con comision calculada, MARCAR PAGADA, persistencia de sesion tras reload, logout limpia token, login afiliado juan@test.com lleva al AffiliateDashboard mostrando SOLO sus ventas y NO ve admin-tabs).
critical_code_review_comments: AdminDashboard.js is ~419 lines — borderline OK but if more tabs grow it should be split into Overview/Affiliates/Sales subcomponents in their own files (currently sub-components live inline in same module).

models.py uses EmailStr = str (alias) to bypass strict email validation. This is intentional per main agent notes but means typos like 'admin@@admin.com' pass through. Consider using pydantic.EmailStr at least for AffiliateCreateIn (admin-only, controlled input) while keeping LoginIn permissive.
auth.py.seed_admin uses os.environ['ADMIN_PASSWORD'] without fallback if .env missing — would crash. .env is currently set so OK, but a defensive default + warning log would harden cold-starts.

affiliates.py /api/sales POST does not validate that the affiliate is `active` before creating a sale; deactivated affiliates can still receive sales via direct API. The UI dropdown filters to active, but defense-in-depth at API level is desirable.
GET /api/stats/network does N+1 query: one find per affiliate to compute breakdown. Fine for <500 affiliates but should switch to a single $group aggregation when scale matters.
JWT_SECRET is hardcoded in /app/backend/.env (committed). For production it should be injected at deploy time, not committed. Acceptable for preview env.

CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False is correct for Bearer-token-only flow (no cookies), but if cookies are later added this would silently fail browsers — leave a comment in server.py.
test_credentials: admin@admin.com / Admin#2026 (admin); juan@test.com / juan123 (affiliate seeded by main agent during iter 2 dev). Both valid at test time. Test created 1 extra affiliate per pytest run (test_aff_<hex>) and 1 UI affiliate (test_ui_<hex>) plus their sales — these accumulate in DB but use unique emails so they do not clash.
seed_data_creation: Test runs leave behind affiliates with prefix 'test_aff_' and 'test_ui_' plus their TEST_Producto sales in MongoDB. No cleanup fixture wired (DB cleanup not strictly required since unique IDs prevent collisions). Main agent may run a one-shot cleanup: db.users.deleteMany({email:/^test_/}) + db.sales.deleteMany({product:/^TEST_/}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_2_affiliates.py as the regression suite for auth + affiliates + sales + stats + iter-1 endpoints. Note: test fixture `juan_token` will skip affiliate-side tests if juan@test.com has not been seeded — re-create him via POST /api/affiliates {name:'Juan Perez', email:'juan@test.com', password:'juan123', commission_pct:25} as admin. Backend lowercases emails on create, so all test fixtures must use lowercase emails (we use 'test_aff_*' not 'TEST_aff_*'). The affiliate dashboard at the public URL correctly hides admin-tabs and only lists rows where affiliate_id matches the logged-in user — verified via UI screenshot.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found. All 28 acceptance criteria from the review request validated. Affiliate isolation enforced both at API (query filters by affiliate_id) and UI (different dashboard component) layers. Commission math verified: amount=100, pct=30 -> commission=30.00 (round to 2 decimals).
